articles.title: This Supernova Showed Up Five Times — and It Could Solve a Giant Space Mystery
articles.summary: A supernova 10 billion light-years away appears as five dots in the sky, thanks to gravity bending its light. Scientists are using this rare cosmic trick to measure how fast the universe is growing. They call it SN Winny.

articles.title: Nuclear Power Is Making a Comeback — But Where Does All the Waste Go?
articles.summary: The U.S. makes 2,000 tons of nuclear waste every year, but there's still no permanent place to store it. Finland is about to open the world's first deep underground storage site. Experts say it's time for other countries to catch up.

articles.title: Want to Think Better? Don't Ask AI for Help Right Away
articles.summary: A study found that people who tried solving a problem on their own before using an AI chatbot scored higher on critical thinking. But when time was short, using AI early helped. It's all about when you ask for help.
